sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.75 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Can a non-abelian one-relator group be the group of all automorphisms of some group? { M.\,V.\,Neshchadim} Yes, it can: in (D.\,J.\,Collins, Proc. London Math. Soc. (3), 36 (1978), no.\,3, 480--493) it was proved that for integers$r,s$such that$(r,s)= 1$,$|r| |s|$, and$r-s$is even, the group$G= a,b a^{-1}b^ra=b^s$is isomorphic to${ Aut}({ Aut} (G))$(V.\,A.\,Churkin, Letter of 5 April 2006\/).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
